rationale: The cited paper focuses on hedonic adaptation in ongoing consumption and shows that interruptions alter experienced intensity, while the citing paper centrally examines satiation/adaptation over repeated hedonic episodes with interspersed stimuli and similarity/categorization manipulations. This strong construct and design overlap (e.g., repeated music/food/hedonic experiences) plus a confirmed DOI match suggests the citation could be theoretically or mechanistically important, not an obvious incidental mention.
